items.title: Photosynthesis (biology primer)
items.doc_text: Photosynthesis is the process by which green plants, algae, and some bacteria convert light energy into chemical energy. It takes place mainly in the chloroplasts, which contain the green pigment chlorophyll. During the light-dependent reactions, water molecules are split, releasing oxygen as a byproduct. The Calvin cycle uses carbon dioxide from the air to build glucose. The overall reaction converts six molecules of carbon dioxide and six molecules of water into one molecule of glucose and six molecules of oxygen.

gold_claims.text: Photosynthesis converts light energy into chemical energy.
It is carried out by green plants, algae, and some bacteria.
It takes place mainly in the chloroplasts.
The light-dependent reactions split water molecules.
Oxygen is released as a byproduct.
The Calvin cycle uses carbon dioxide to build glucose.
The overall reaction yields glucose from six carbon dioxide and six water.

items.title: The water cycle (earth science primer)
items.doc_text: The water cycle describes how water moves continuously through the Earth and its atmosphere. Evaporation turns liquid water from oceans and lakes into water vapor. As the vapor rises and cools, condensation forms clouds. When the droplets grow heavy enough, precipitation falls as rain or snow. Water that reaches the ground can flow into rivers through runoff, before eventually returning to the ocean. The Sun provides the energy that drives the entire water cycle.

gold_claims.text: Water moves through the Earth and its atmosphere.
Evaporation turns liquid water into water vapor.
Water evaporates from oceans and lakes.
Precipitation falls as rain or snow.
Water flows into rivers through runoff.
Water eventually returns to the ocean.
The Sun provides the energy that drives the cycle.

decisions.label: Custody proves provenance of the recorded run — not correctness, not reproducibility. An LLM verdict is a signal, never a custody proof.
Pass bar fixed BEFORE running: precision ≥ 0.80 AND recall ≥ 0.75
Selected google/gemma-3-27b-it — cheapest passing model on this corpus

detail.reason: cheapest model meeting precision>=0.80 and recall>=0.75 on this corpus

decisions.label: Trusted model = the cheapest model that clears the pass bar
The 70B Llama emitted invalid JSON on 1 of 2 docs — unusable by an automated pipeline
Every model that returned valid output scored 100% precision — no ungrounded spans survived
Model size did NOT predict recall: 27B Gemma (0.9375) beat the 70B and 120B models

citation.title: Fractal Custody Objects: route-comparable chain-of-custody for deterministic computational biology and AI-agent provenance
